# Field of view and fog of war requirements (2026-02-28 09:59Z)

_**User**_

Check if Phase 2.2 Field of View & Fog of War requirements are met:

Requirements:
- [x] Implement FOVSystem using ROT.FOV.PreciseShadowcasting
- [x] Calculate visible tiles from player position with radius 8
- [x] Three tile states: unexplored (black), explored-but-not-visible (dim), visible (full brightness)
- [x] Update FOV on every player move
- [x] Apply as alpha/tint on Phaser tilemap layers
- [x] Store explored tiles in game state (persist within session)
- [x] Emit 'area-entered' event when new room is revealed

Check src/game/systems/FOVSystem.ts and src/game/scenes/ExploreScene.ts.

Report which requirements are met (✅) or not met (❌) with brief evidence.

---


## Phase 2.2 Field of View & Fog of War Requirements Check

### ✅ Implement FOVSystem using ROT.FOV.PreciseShadowcasting
**Evidence:** `FOVSystem.ts` line 7 imports `FOV` from 'rot-js', line 41 instantiates `new FOV.PreciseShadowcasting(lightPasses)`.

### ✅ Calculate visible tiles from player position with radius 8
**Evidence:** `gameConfig.ts` line 9 defines `FOV_RADIUS = 8`. `FOVSystem.ts` line 32 defaults `radius = FOV_RADIUS`. `ExploreScene.ts` line 549 calls `computeVisibleTiles` with the radius parameter. Note: actual radius varies with battery (3-8) via `getFovRadiusFromBattery`, but the system supports radius 8.

### ✅ Three tile states: unexplored (black), explored-but-not-visible (dim), visible (full brightness)
**Evidence:** `ExploreScene.ts` lines 573-595 (`applyFogOfWar`):
- Line 586: Visible tiles skipped (full brightness)
- Line 590: `const alpha = explored.has(coord) ? 0.5 : 1;` — explored-but-not-visible = 0.5 alpha (dim), unexplored = 1.0 alpha (black)

### ✅ Update FOV on every player move
**Evidence:** `ExploreScene.ts` lines 139-147: `onPlayerMoved` callback calls `this.updateFOV(x, y)` on every move. Also called on initial spawn (line 186).

### ⚠️ Apply as alpha/tint on Phaser tilemap layers (PARTIAL)
**Evidence:** Uses a fog overlay Graphics object instead of modifying tilemap layers:
- Lines 109-111: Creates `fogOverlay` Graphics object
- Lines 573-595: Draws fog rectangles on the overlay
This matches the project rules (avoids Phaser layer API issues), but it's not "alpha/tint on tilemap layers" as stated. Functionally equivalent.

### ✅ Store explored tiles in game state (persist within session)
**Evidence:** `gameStore.ts`:
- Lines 232-238: `addExploredTiles` adds tiles to `session.exploredTiles`
- Line 288: `exploredTiles: []` is in session state
- Lines 299-303: `partialize` shows session is not persisted (only player, library, exploration are persisted)
- Line 184: `clearExploredTiles()` called on new map
Session-only storage matches "persist within session".

### ✅ Emit 'area-entered' event when new room is revealed
**Evidence:** `ExploreScene.ts`:
- Lines 561-570: Loop through visible tiles, check room names, emit `'area-entered'` when a new room enters visibility
- Line 542: Also emits on room entry (when `lastRoomName` changes)
- `EventBridge.ts` line 18 defines the event type

---

**Summary:** 6/7 requirements fully met, 1 partially met (fog overlay instead of tilemap layer modification, but functionally equivalent and aligned with project rules).

---
